preamble: The nasalized vowel [ã] denasalizes to the regular vowel [a] through loss of nasalization. This phonological process is documented in Romance languages, where nasalization is lost through weakening or phonotactic environments that disfavor nasalization.

examples.note: Denasalization of [ã] to [a] in certain phonological contexts or younger speakers' speech patterns.
Historical denasalization pattern in Portuguese dialects and speech variation.

context: Before /m/, /n/, /ŋ/ in English; nasality is transferred to the following consonant
allophones_note: Allophonic nasalization in English occurs through vowel-consonant coarticulation
